description_long: This object is a tree trunk with a thick, robust base and prominent roots spreading outward onto a patch of earth. The trunk is mostly straight, with a slightly irregular surface and subtle bulges, and is covered in a textured bark that transitions from a dark brown at the base to a lighter, mossy green higher up. The roots are gnarled and extend visibly above the ground, merging into a rough, uneven patch of soil that is dotted with moss and organic debris. There are no visible branches or foliage, giving the impression of a mature tree that has been pruned or cut, leaving only the trunk and root system. The surface of the trunk and roots appears hard and rough, with a matte finish and natural color variations. This object would be well-suited for placement in a park, garden, or naturalistic urban setting.
description: A thick, mossy tree trunk with exposed roots spreading over a rough patch of earth, lacking branches or foliage. The bark is textured and transitions from dark brown to green, with a rugged, natural appearance.
description_view_0: The trunk is seen from the front, showing a prominent root system spreading outwards and a slightly bulging, mossy surface.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the roots extending more to the left, with the trunk's surface showing a smoother, lighter green area.
description_view_2: The back view reveals the widest spread of roots and a lighter, almost grayish section of bark near the top.
description_view_3: This angle shows a more vertical trunk with roots fanning out to the left, and a darker, more textured bark surface.
attribute_pot_type: No visible pot; the trunk and roots are set directly into a patch of naturalistic soil.
attribute_trunk_curvature: The trunk is mostly straight with minor irregularities and subtle bulges, resembling a formal upright style but without pronounced curvature or twists.
attribute_branch_arrangement: No branches are present; the object consists solely of the trunk and exposed roots.
attribute_leaf_density: No foliage is present, resulting in a sparse appearance with only the trunk and roots visible.
